title: 3 takeaways from the Revolution’s 3-0 loss to Nashville SC
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: New England Revolution's seven-game unbeaten streak ended with a 3-0 loss to Nashville SC at Gillette Stadium.
Bryan Acosta scored twice, Warren Madrigal once, and Matt Turner's clearance error led to Nashville's second goal.
With injuries to key players, New England struggled to create chances and slipped to third in the Eastern Conference.

title: Massport to attempt Guinness World Record for world’s largest soccer ball
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Massport plans to unveil a 45-foot-tall soccer ball in East Boston to break the Guinness World Record.
The current record for the largest soccer ball is 38 feet 11.8 inches, set in Qatar in 2013.
The installation will be at Piers Park II from June 12-18, coinciding with Boston's FIFA World Cup initiatives.

title: Famous makeup artist gets backlash for mocking former Spirit Airlines employee at Logan over fundraiser
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Makeup influencer James Charles criticized former Spirit Airlines employee Amber Lendof Vargas for a GoFundMe request after job loss.
Spirit Airlines abruptly shut down, leaving 17,000 workers, including Vargas at Logan Airport, jobless.
Following backlash, James Charles donated $5,000 to Vargas's fundraiser, acknowledging previous comments were privileged and unnecessary.

title: Photos: State trooper killed by wrong-way driver in Lynnfield crash laid to rest
author: Audrey Cooney
bullets: Trooper Kevin Trainor, 30, was killed by a wrong-way driver in Lynnfield on May 6, 2026.
Funeral services were held at St. James Church in Salem for Trainor, who grew up in the area.
Hernan Marrero, the wrong-way driver from Roslindale, also died in the crash, according to officials.

title: Angell Animal Medical Center treating cat who fell 10 stories
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Mazy, a one-year-old cat, survived a ten-story fall from a Greater Boston apartment building.
She sustained broken front legs and a collapsed lung; surgery will be performed at Angell Animal Medical Center.
The MSPCA-Angell is collecting donations to cover Mazy's medical expenses, estimated to be up to $15,000.

title: AG says auditor can hire attorney to force audit of Legislature after SJC order
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Attorney General Andrea Campbell permits Auditor Diana DiZoglio to hire attorney for Legislature audit after SJC's directive.
SJC sets 30-day deadline for AG to determine representation for DiZoglio's narrowed audit scope focusing on Senate finances.
Auditor DiZoglio prioritizes Legislature audit following voter approval; attorney Shannon Liss-Riordan to represent the case.

title: Kyle Schwarber reveals tepid pursuit from Red Sox this past offseason
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Kyle Schwarber discussed the Red Sox's limited offseason interest before re-signing with the Phillies for $150 million over five years.
Since leaving the Red Sox, Schwarber has hit 204 home runs in 669 games with the Phillies, excelling as a power hitter.
Schwarber performs well at Fenway Park, holding a career OPS of 1.137, ranking fifth all-time among players with 100+ plate appearances.

title: Jayson Tatum reveals new goal he’s chasing with the Celtics
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Jayson Tatum aims to win another championship and secure the Finals MVP with the Boston Celtics in the 2026-27 season.
Tatum, 28, missed most of the 2025-26 NBA season due to a ruptured Achilles tendon injury.
Despite knee soreness in the playoffs, Tatum averaged 21.8 points upon his return from injury last season.

title: 7 contracts Bruins might try to unload this offseason
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Boston Bruins could clear over $16 million in cap space by trading certain contracts this offseason.
Potential trade candidates include Joonas Korpisalo, Henri Jokiharju, and Elias Lindholm among others.
Lindholm's contract poses challenges due to his injuries and no-movement clause, complicating potential trades.

title: First white shark sighting of 2026 confirmed off Massachusetts coast
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: A dead gray seal with suspected shark bite wounds was found on Martha's Vineyard, prompting white shark confirmation.
The New England Aquarium confirmed the first white shark sighting of the season off the Massachusetts coast on May 10.
Officials advise beachgoers to stay alert, avoid seal areas, and report shark sightings via the Sharktivity app.
